title: The AI paradox: More automation, more humans, more work | Dan Shipper
summary: Why most work will happen inside Codex or Claude Code, the CLI era is over, every agent needs a human, and why he’s wildly bullish on PMs and designers
score_reason: High content potential with a clear practitioner perspective on AI's impact on work roles and human collaboration, offering insight and trend read valuable to product managers and designers.
partner_takeaway: AI automation increases the need for human roles like product managers and designers, emphasizing collaboration rather than replacement.
partner_summary: This article explores the paradox of AI automation increasing human work, focusing on tools like Codex and Claude Code and the end of the CLI era. It matters because it challenges common assumptions about AI replacing jobs and highlights the growing importance of PMs and designers in AI-driven workflows. Readers should reconsider how they integrate AI in their teams and prepare for evolving collaboration models between humans and AI agents.
